content.summaryText: PPE Selection and Donning in Prevention and Infection Control focuses on transmission prevention through hygiene and PPE. Primary objective: Match PPE to exposure level.

content.questionText: In PPE Selection and Donning, which action best supports 'Match PPE to exposure level'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Perform hand hygiene and select PPE by transmission route
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: perform hand hygiene and select PPE by transmission route. Practical emphasis: Don and doff safely.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when clustered cases, high-risk exposure, or protocol breach.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
